content_en:
Based upon this, if a person began to pray Wifr with the intention of offering two Rak‘ahs, then he made the Taslim and then offered a third Rak‘ah, but he forgot and stood up for the third Rak‘ah without making the Taslim, then we say to him: Complete the third Rak‘ah, because it is permissible in Witr to exceed two Rak‘ahs.

Q. 274. A worshipper in prayer stood up instead of sitting for the Tashahhud and before he started the recitation, he remembered, should he sit down in Tashahhud? And when should he perform the prostration of forgetfulness in this situation, before the Taslim, or after it?

A. In this situation, he should not go back, because he has completely left the Tashahhud since he has reached the next pillar. It is therefore disliked for him to go back. Although going back does not invalidate his prayer, because he has not done anything unlawful. However, he must perform the prostration of forgetfulness and it should be before the Taslim.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 507 Beall ogi

Q. 275. What is the ruling on Witr, and is it something peculiar to Ramadan?

A. Witr is a compulsory Sunnah in Ramadan and at other times, so much so that Imam Ahmad and others said: Whoever abandons Witr then he is a bad man, and his testimony should not be accepted.

So, it is a compulsory Sunnah, and it is not befitting for a Muslim to abandon it neither in Ramadan, nor at any other time and Witr is to seal the night prayer with a single Rak‘ah; it is not, as some of the common people think, Qunut. Qunut is one thing, and Witr is another: Witr is that one seals the night prayer with a single Rak‘ah or three together.

In any case, Witr is a compulsory Sunnah in Ramadan and in other months, so it is not befitting for a Muslim to abandon it.

Q. 276. We request that Your Eminence describe the Sunnah regarding the supplication of Qunut and tell us if there are special! supplications for it? And is it prescribed to make it long in the Witr prayer?

A. Included in the supplication of Qunut is that which was taught by the Prophet # to Hasan bin ‘Ali bin Abu Talib, may Allah be pleased with them:

(Allahummahdini Feeman Hadait, Wa ‘Afini Feeman ‘Afait.) “Oh, Allah! Guide me among those whont You guide and pardon me among those whom You pardon.’""}

"I Reported by At-Tirmithi, in the Chapters on Prayer, Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding Qunut in Witr (464).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 508 pe YI OS) Gold

Allahummahdina (Oh, Allah! Guide us) using the first person plural pronoun, because he is supplicating for himself and for those behind him.

If he said anything suitable, there is no sin, but it is not fitting for him to prolong the supplication to such an extent that it becomes difficult for those being led in prayer, or causing them to become bored. The Prophet #8 became angry with Mu’ath, may Allah be pleased with him, when he prolonged the prayer with his people and said:

“Are you one who is a cause of trial, oh, Mu‘ath?.")

Q. 277. Is it part of the Sunnah to raise the hands when making the supplication of Qunut, and what is the evidence for that?

A. Yes, itis part of the Sunnah for a person to raise his hands when he makes the supplication of Qunut. This has been reported from the Messenger of Allah #8 in his Qunut, when he used to perform Qunut in the obligatory prayers at times of calamity. Likewise, it has been authentically reported from the Commander of the Faithful, ‘Umar bin Al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, that he raised his hands in the Qunut of Witr and he was one of the righteous caliphs whom we were ordered to follow.

So, raising the hands when making the Qunut of Witr is a Sunnah whether it be for an Imam or one who is led in prayer, or a single worshipper. Therefore, whenever you make Qunut, raise your hands.

Q. 278. What is the ruling on Qunut in the obligatory prayers? And what is the ruling if the Muslims are afflicted by some calamity?

"1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: Whoever Complained of His Imam if He Prolonged the Prayer (705).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 509 Bal is gk

A. Qunut in the obligatory prayers is not legislated, and it is not desirable that it should be done. However, if the Imam performs Qunut, you should follow him, because discord is evil.

If the Muslims are afflicted by some calamity, then there is no objection in that case to their making Qunut in order to ask Allah, the Most High to remove it.

Q. 279. What is the ruling on Tarawih and how many Rak‘ahs is it?

A. The Tarawih prayer is a Sunnah which was prescribed by the Messenger of Allah #% and it is reported in the Two Sahihs on the authority of ‘Aishah, may Allah be pleased with her, that the Prophet #% prayed in the Masjid one night and some people prayed with him. Then he prayed on the next night and the people had become numerous, then they gathered on the third night or the fourth night, but the Messenger of Allah 2% did not come out. Then in the morning, he said:

Ff) 8 Ley at eres if odes @ of ec SY SS Al & pee Ab epee gill Ci15 Sao “I saw what you did, and nothing prevented me from coming out to you except that I feared it would become an obligation upon

As for the number of Rak‘ahs, it is eleven Rak‘ahs according to what has been reported in the the Two Sahihs on the authority of ‘Aishah, may Allah be pleased with her. She was asked how the prayer of the Prophet # was in Ramadan, and she said: “He did not pray more than eleven Rak‘ahs, neither in Ramadan, nor in

"1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Tahajjud, in the Chapter: The Encouragement of the Prophet #% to Offer the Night Prayer (1129); and by Muslim in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: The Encouragement of Offering the Night Prayer in Ramadan (177).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 510 eeY! Oolsyi Gas

If one prayed thirteen Rak‘ahs, then there is no objection, according to the words of Ibn ‘Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them: “The Prayer of the Prophet #¢ was thirteen Rak‘ahs. that is, the night prayer.”7!

Eleven Rak‘ahs is what has been confirmed from ‘Umar bin AlKhattab, may Allah be pleased with him, as reported in (Imam Malik’s) Al-Muwatta’ with a chain of narrators that is one of the most authentic of chains.!*!

If one prayed more than this, then there is no objection, according to the words of the Prophet #¢, when he was asked about the night prayer, he said:

He did not define a number. A number of different forms have been reported from the Salaf, and there is room for choosing in the matter. But it is preferable to restrict oneself to what has been reported from the Prophet #%, which is eleven Rak’ahs, or thirteen Rak‘ahs.

1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Tahajjud, in the Chapter: The Night Prayer of the Prophet # (1147); and by Muslim in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: The Night Prayer (125).

'2) Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Tahajjud, in the Chapter: The Manner of the Prophet's Prayer, Hadith (1138); and it was reported by Muslim in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: The Supplication in the Night Prayer, Hadith 194 (764).

'3) Reported by Imam Malik in the Book of Prayers, in the Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding the Night Prayer in Ramadan 1:110 (280).

'] Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Witr, in the Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding Witr (990); and by Muslim in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: The Night Prayer Two by Two (145).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 511 Beall cyl

It has not been authentically reported from the Prophet #% that he or any of the caliphs that they prayed twenty-three; rather what is confirmed from ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, is that he prayed eleven Rak‘ahs, since he ordered Ubayy bin Ka’b and Tamim Ad-Dari, may Allah be pleased with them, to stand for the people and pray eleven Rak‘ahs.!"!

This is what was deemed proper by such as ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, that his way in this matter be that of the Messenger of Allah ¢.

We are unaware that any of the Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, prayed more than twenty-three Rak‘ahs; indeed it is clear that the opposite is the case. And we have already mentioned the saying of ‘Aishah, may Allah be pleased with her: “He did not pray more than eleven Rak‘ahs, neither in Ramadan, nor in any other month.”

As for the consensus of the Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, there is no doubt that it is an evidence, because among them are the righteous caliphs whom the Prophet 2 ordered us to follow, and because they are the best of generations from this (Muslim) nation.

You should know that the difference of opinion regarding the number of Rak‘ahs in Tarawih and the like is in a matter in which it is permissible to exercise Ijtihad. It is undesirable that it should be a cause of differing and schism between the people, especially since the Salaf differed in the matter. There is nothing in the matter to prevent the exercise of Ijtihad. How fine is the saying of one of the scholars, when a person differed with him in his Ijtihad in a matter in which it was permissible: By _ contradicting me, you have agreed with me, for both of us consider that it is obligatory to follow what one sees to be the

''l Reported by Malik in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter on the Night Prayer in Ramadan (280).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 512 PRY OI I sobs

truth, since it is permissible to exercise Jjtihad. We ask Allah, the Most High that He grant us all success in attaining that which He loves and is pleasing to Him.

Q. 280. What is the ruling on making the supplication for completing the recitation of the Qur’an during the night prayer in the month of Ramadan?
content_id:
Berdasarkan hal ini, jika seseorang memulai salat Witir dengan niat mengerjakan dua rakaat, lalu ia melakukan taslim dan kemudian mengerjakan rakaat ketiga, namun ia lupa dan berdiri untuk rakaat ketiga tanpa melakukan taslim, maka kita katakan kepadanya: Sempurnakanlah rakaat ketiga, karena dalam Witir diperbolehkan lebih dari dua rakaat.

T. 274. Seorang musalli dalam salat berdiri padahal seharusnya duduk untuk tasyahhud, dan sebelum ia memulai bacaan, ia teringat, haruskah ia duduk kembali untuk tasyahhud? Dan kapan ia harus melakukan sujud sahwi dalam situasi ini, sebelum taslim atau sesudahnya?

J. Dalam situasi ini, ia tidak boleh kembali, karena ia telah meninggalkan tasyahhud sepenuhnya karena telah mencapai rukun berikutnya. Oleh karena itu, dimakruhkan baginya untuk kembali. Meskipun kembali tidak membatalkan salatnya, karena ia tidak melakukan sesuatu yang haram. Namun, ia wajib melakukan sujud sahwi dan hendaknya dilakukan sebelum taslim.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 507

T. 275. Apa hukum Witir, dan apakah ia khusus untuk Ramadan saja?

J. Witir adalah Sunnah muakkadah di bulan Ramadan dan di waktu lainnya, sampai-sampai Imam Ahmad dan ulama lainnya berkata: Barangsiapa yang meninggalkan Witir maka ia adalah orang yang buruk, dan persaksiannya tidak boleh diterima.

Jadi, Witir adalah Sunnah muakkadah, dan tidak layak bagi seorang Muslim untuk meninggalkannya baik di bulan Ramadan maupun di waktu lainnya. Witir adalah menutup salat malam dengan satu rakaat; bukan seperti yang dikira sebagian orang awam bahwa Witir itu adalah qunut. Qunut adalah satu hal, dan Witir adalah hal lain: Witir adalah menutup salat malam dengan satu rakaat atau tiga rakaat secara bersamaan.

Bagaimanapun, Witir adalah Sunnah muakkadah di bulan Ramadan dan di bulan-bulan lainnya, maka tidak layak bagi seorang Muslim untuk meninggalkannya.

T. 276. Kami memohon kepada Yang Mulia untuk menjelaskan Sunnah mengenai du'a qunut dan memberi tahu kami apakah ada du'a khusus untuknya? Dan apakah disyariatkan untuk memanjangkannya dalam salat Witir?

J. Termasuk dalam du'a qunut adalah apa yang diajarkan oleh Nabi ﷺ kepada Hasan bin 'Ali bin Abu Talib radhiyallahu 'anhuma:

(Allahummahdini Feeman Hadait, Wa 'Afini Feeman 'Afait.) "Ya Allah! Berilah aku petunjuk di antara orang-orang yang Engkau beri petunjuk, dan berilah aku keselamatan di antara orang-orang yang Engkau beri keselamatan."

Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi, dalam Kitab Salat, Bab: Apa yang Diriwayatkan Mengenai Qunut dalam Witir (464).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 508

Allahummahdina (Ya Allah! Berilah kami petunjuk) menggunakan kata ganti orang pertama jamak, karena ia berdu'a untuk dirinya dan untuk orang-orang di belakangnya.

Jika ia menambahkan du'a yang sesuai, maka tidak ada dosa. Namun tidak layak baginya untuk memanjangkan du'a sampai memberatkan para makmum atau membuat mereka bosan. Nabi ﷺ marah kepada Mu'adz radhiyallahu 'anhu ketika ia memanjangkan salat bersama kaumnya dan bersabda:

"Apakah engkau orang yang menyebabkan fitnah, wahai Mu'adz?"

T. 277. Apakah termasuk Sunnah mengangkat tangan ketika berdu'a qunut, dan apa dalilnya?

J. Ya, termasuk Sunnah bagi seseorang untuk mengangkat tangannya ketika berdu'a qunut. Hal ini telah diriwayatkan dari Rasulullah ﷺ dalam qunutnya, ketika beliau melakukan qunut dalam salat wajib pada saat terjadi musibah. Demikian pula, telah diriwayatkan secara sahih dari Amirul Mukminin, 'Umar bin Al-Khaththab radhiyallahu 'anhu, bahwa beliau mengangkat tangannya dalam qunut Witir, dan beliau adalah salah satu khalifah yang saleh yang kita diperintahkan untuk mengikutinya.

Jadi, mengangkat tangan ketika berdu'a qunut Witir adalah Sunnah, baik bagi imam, makmum, maupun orang yang salat sendirian. Oleh karena itu, setiap kali engkau melakukan qunut, angkatlah tanganmu.

T. 278. Apa hukum qunut dalam salat wajib? Dan apa hukumnya jika kaum Muslimin ditimpa musibah?

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, Bab: Barangsiapa yang Mengeluhkan Imamnya Jika Ia Memanjangkan Salat (705).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 509

J. Qunut dalam salat wajib tidak disyariatkan, dan tidak disunahkan untuk melakukannya. Namun jika imam melakukan qunut, hendaknya engkau mengikutinya, karena perpecahan itu adalah keburukan.

Jika kaum Muslimin ditimpa musibah, maka tidak ada halangan dalam hal itu untuk melakukan qunut guna memohon kepada Allah Ta'ala agar menghilangkannya.

T. 279. Apa hukum Tarawih dan berapa jumlah rakaatnya?

J. Salat Tarawih adalah Sunnah yang disyariatkan oleh Rasulullah ﷺ. Diriwayatkan dalam Shahih Al-Bukhari dan Muslim dari 'Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha, bahwa Nabi ﷺ salat di masjid pada suatu malam dan beberapa orang ikut salat bersamanya. Kemudian beliau salat pada malam berikutnya dan orang-orang semakin banyak, lalu mereka berkumpul pada malam ketiga atau keempat, namun Rasulullah ﷺ tidak keluar. Kemudian di pagi harinya, beliau bersabda:

"Aku telah melihat apa yang kalian lakukan, dan tidak ada yang menghalangiku untuk keluar menemui kalian kecuali aku khawatir hal itu akan menjadi kewajiban atas kalian."

Adapun jumlah rakaatnya, yaitu sebelas rakaat berdasarkan apa yang diriwayatkan dalam Shahih Al-Bukhari dan Muslim dari 'Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha. Beliau ditanya tentang salat Nabi ﷺ di bulan Ramadan, dan beliau menjawab: "Beliau tidak pernah salat lebih dari sebelas rakaat, baik di bulan Ramadan maupun di

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Tahajjud, Bab: Anjuran Nabi ﷺ untuk Melakukan Salat Malam (1129); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat Musafir, Bab: Anjuran Melakukan Salat Malam di Bulan Ramadan (177).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 510

Jika seseorang salat tiga belas rakaat, maka tidak ada halangan, berdasarkan perkataan Ibnu 'Abbas radhiyallahu 'anhuma: "Salat Nabi ﷺ adalah tiga belas rakaat, yaitu salat malam."

Sebelas rakaat adalah yang telah dikonfirmasi dari 'Umar bin Al-Khaththab radhiyallahu 'anhu, sebagaimana diriwayatkan dalam Al-Muwaththa' (Imam Malik) dengan sanad yang termasuk sanad paling sahih.

Jika seseorang salat lebih dari itu, maka tidak ada halangan, berdasarkan sabda Nabi ﷺ ketika ditanya tentang salat malam, beliau bersabda:

Beliau tidak menentukan jumlah tertentu. Berbagai bentuk yang berbeda telah diriwayatkan dari para Salaf, dan ada kelapangan dalam memilih dalam masalah ini. Namun yang lebih utama adalah membatasi diri pada apa yang diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ, yaitu sebelas rakaat atau tiga belas rakaat.

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Tahajjud, Bab: Salat Malam Nabi ﷺ (1147); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat Musafir, Bab: Salat Malam (125).

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Tahajjud, Bab: Tata Cara Salat Nabi ﷺ, Hadits (1138); dan diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat Musafir, Bab: Du'a dalam Salat Malam, Hadits 194 (764).

Diriwayatkan oleh Imam Malik dalam Kitab Salat, Bab: Apa yang Diriwayatkan Mengenai Salat Malam di Bulan Ramadan 1:110 (280).

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Witir, Bab: Apa yang Diriwayatkan Mengenai Witir (990); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat, Bab: Salat Malam Dua-Dua (145).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 511

Tidak diriwayatkan secara sahih dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau atau salah satu khalifah pernah salat dua puluh tiga rakaat; bahkan yang dikonfirmasi dari 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhu adalah bahwa beliau salat sebelas rakaat, karena beliau memerintahkan Ubayy bin Ka'b dan Tamim Ad-Dari radhiyallahu 'anhuma untuk memimpin orang-orang dan salat sebelas rakaat.

Inilah yang dianggap tepat oleh seorang seperti 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhu, bahwa jalannya dalam masalah ini adalah jalan Rasulullah ﷺ.

Kami tidak mengetahui bahwa ada seorang Sahabat pun radhiyallahu 'anhum yang salat lebih dari dua puluh tiga rakaat; bahkan yang jelas adalah sebaliknya. Dan kami telah menyebutkan perkataan 'Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha: "Beliau tidak pernah salat lebih dari sebelas rakaat, baik di bulan Ramadan maupun di bulan lainnya."

Adapun ijma' (kesepakatan) para Sahabat, semoga Allah meridhai mereka, tidak diragukan lagi bahwa itu adalah hujjah, karena di antara mereka terdapat para khalifah yang saleh yang Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan kita untuk mengikuti mereka, dan karena mereka adalah generasi terbaik dari umat (Islam) ini.

Ketahuilah bahwa perbedaan pendapat mengenai jumlah rakaat dalam Tarawih dan sejenisnya adalah dalam perkara yang diperbolehkan untuk berijtihad. Tidak selayaknya hal itu menjadi sebab perpecahan dan perselisihan di antara manusia, terutama karena para Salaf sendiri berbeda pendapat dalam masalah ini. Tidak ada sesuatu pun dalam masalah ini yang menghalangi pelaksanaan ijtihad. Betapa indahnya ucapan salah seorang ulama, ketika seseorang berbeda pendapat dengannya dalam ijtihadnya pada perkara yang diperbolehkan: Dengan menyelisihiku, engkau justru telah sepakat denganku, karena kita berdua berpandangan bahwa wajib mengikuti apa yang diyakini sebagai

''l Diriwayatkan oleh Malik dalam Kitab Salat, pada Bab Salat Malam di Bulan Ramadan (280).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 512 PRY OI I sobs

kebenaran, karena ijtihad diperbolehkan dalam hal ini. Kita memohon kepada Allah Ta'ala agar memberikan taufik kepada kita semua untuk meraih apa yang dicintai-Nya dan diridhai-Nya.

Q. 280. Apa hukum membaca du'a khatam Al-Quran dalam salat malam di bulan Ramadan?
content_ar: Based upon this, if a person began to pray Wifr with the intention of offering two Rak‘ahs, then he made the Taslim and then offered a third Rak‘ah, but he forgot and stood up for the third Rak‘ah without making the Taslim, then we say to him: Complete the third Rak‘ah, because it is مباح in Witr to exceed two Rak‘ahs. Q. 274. A worshipper in الصلاة stood up instead of sitting for the Tashahhud and before he started the recitation, he remembered, should he sit down in Tashahhud? And when should he perform the prostration of forgetfulness in this situation, before the Taslim, or after it? A. In this situation, he should not go back, because he has completely left the Tashahhud since he has reached the next pillar. It is therefore مكروه for him to go back. Although going back does not invalidate his الصلاة, because he has not done anything unlawful. However, he must perform the prostration of forgetfulness and it should be before the Taslim. الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 507 Beall ogi Q. 275. What is the ruling on Witr, and is it something peculiar to Ramadan? A. Witr is a واجب السنة in Ramadan and at other times, so much so that الإمام Ahmad and others said: Whoever abandons Witr then he is a bad man, and his testimony should not be accepted. So, it is a واجب السنة, and it is not befitting for a المسلم to abandon it neither in Ramadan, nor at any other time and Witr is to seal the night الصلاة with a single Rak‘ah; it is not, as some of the common people think, Qunut. Qunut is one thing, and Witr is another: Witr is that one seals the night الصلاة with a single Rak‘ah or three together. In any case, Witr is a واجب السنة in Ramadan and in other months, so it is not befitting for a المسلم to abandon it. Q. 276. We request that Your Eminence describe السنة النبوية regarding the الدعاء of Qunut and tell us if there are special! supplications for it? And is it prescribed to make it long in the Witr الصلاة? A. Included in the الدعاء of Qunut is that which was taught by النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # to Hasan bin ‘Ali bin Abu Talib, رضي الله عنهم: (Allahummahdini Feeman Hadait, Wa ‘Afini Feeman ‘Afait.) “Oh, الله! Guide me among those whont You guide and pardon me among those whom You pardon.’""} "I Reported by At-Tirmithi, in the Chapters on الصلاة, Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding Qunut in Witr (464). الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 508 pe YI OS) Gold Allahummahdina (Oh, الله! Guide us) using the first person plural pronoun, because he is supplicating for himself and for those behind him. If he said anything suitable, there is no sin, but it is not fitting for him to prolong the الدعاء to such an extent that it becomes difficult for those being led in الصلاة, or causing them to become bored. النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #8 became angry with Mu’ath, رضي الله عنه, when he prolonged the الصلاة with his people and said: “Are you one who is a cause of trial, oh, Mu‘ath?.") Q. 277. Is it part of السنة النبوية to raise the hands when making the الدعاء of Qunut, and what is the evidence for that? A. Yes, itis part of السنة النبوية for a person to raise his hands when he makes the الدعاء of Qunut. This has been reported from رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم #8 in his Qunut, when he used to perform Qunut in the واجب prayers at times of calamity. Likewise, it has been authentically reported from the Commander of the Faithful, ‘Umar bin Al-Khattab, رضي الله عنه, that he raised his hands in the Qunut of Witr and he was one of the righteous caliphs whom we were ordered to follow. So, raising the hands when making the Qunut of Witr is a السنة whether it be for an الإمام or one who is led in الصلاة, or a single worshipper. Therefore, whenever you make Qunut, raise your hands. Q. 278. What is the ruling on Qunut in the واجب prayers? And what is the ruling if the المسلمون are afflicted by some calamity? "1 Reported by البخاري in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: Whoever Complained of His الإمام if He Prolonged the الصلاة (705). الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 509 Bal is gk A. Qunut in the واجب prayers is not legislated, and it is not مستحب that it should be done. However, if the الإمام performs Qunut, you should follow him, because discord is evil. If the المسلمون are afflicted by some calamity, then there is no objection in that case to their making Qunut in order to ask الله, the Most High to remove it. Q. 279. What is the ruling on Tarawih and how many Rak‘ahs is it? A. The Tarawih الصلاة is a السنة which was prescribed by رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم #% and it is reported in the Two Sahihs on the authority of ‘Aishah, رضي الله عنها, that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% prayed in the المسجد one night and some people prayed with him. Then he prayed on the next night and the people had become numerous, then they gathered on the third night or the fourth night, but رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم 2% did not come out. Then in the morning, he said: Ff) 8 Ley at eres if odes @ of ec SY SS Al & pee Ab epee gill Ci15 Sao “I saw what you did, and nothing prevented me from coming out to you except that I feared it would become an obligation upon As for the number of Rak‘ahs, it is eleven Rak‘ahs according to what has been reported in the the Two Sahihs on the authority of ‘Aishah, رضي الله عنها. She was asked how the الصلاة of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # was in Ramadan, and she said: “He did not pray more than eleven Rak‘ahs, neither in Ramadan, nor in "1 Reported by البخاري in the Book of Tahajjud, in the Chapter: The Encouragement of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% to Offer the Night الصلاة (1129); and by المسلم in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: The Encouragement of Offering the Night الصلاة in Ramadan (177). الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 510 eeY! Oolsyi Gas If one prayed thirteen Rak‘ahs, then there is no objection, according to the words of Ibn ‘Abbas, رضي الله عنهم: “The الصلاة of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #¢ was thirteen Rak‘ahs. that is, the night الصلاة.”7! Eleven Rak‘ahs is what has been confirmed from ‘Umar bin AlKhattab, رضي الله عنه, as reported in (الإمام Malik’s) Al-Muwatta’ with a chain of narrators that is one of the most صحيح of chains.!*! If one prayed more than this, then there is no objection, according to the words of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #¢, when he was asked about the night الصلاة, he said: He did not define a number. A number of different forms have been reported from the Salaf, and there is room for choosing in the matter. But it is preferable to restrict oneself to what has been reported from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #%, which is eleven Rak’ahs, or thirteen Rak‘ahs. 1 Reported by البخاري in the Book of Tahajjud, in the Chapter: The Night الصلاة of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # (1147); and by المسلم in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: The Night الصلاة (125). '2) Reported by البخاري in the Book of Tahajjud, in the Chapter: The Manner of the Prophet's الصلاة, الحديث (1138); and it was reported by المسلم in the Book of the Travellers’ Prayers, in the Chapter: The الدعاء in the Night الصلاة, الحديث 194 (764). '3) Reported by الإمام Malik in the Book of Prayers, in the Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding the Night الصلاة in Ramadan 1:110 (280). '] Reported by البخاري in the Book of Witr, in the Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding Witr (990); and by المسلم in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: The Night الصلاة Two by Two (145). الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 511 Beall cyl It has not been authentically reported from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% that he or any of the caliphs that they prayed twenty-three; rather what is confirmed from ‘Umar, رضي الله عنه, is that he prayed eleven Rak‘ahs, since he ordered Ubayy bin Ka’b and Tamim Ad-Dari, رضي الله عنهم, to stand for the people and pray eleven Rak‘ahs.!"! This is what was deemed proper by such as ‘Umar, رضي الله عنه, that his way in this matter be that of رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ¢. We are unaware that any of الصحابة الكرام رضي الله عنهم, رضي الله عنهم, prayed more than twenty-three Rak‘ahs; indeed it is clear that the opposite is the case. And we have already mentioned the saying of ‘Aishah, رضي الله عنها: “He did not pray more than eleven Rak‘ahs, neither in Ramadan, nor in any other month.” As for the الإجماع of الصحابة الكرام رضي الله عنهم, رضي الله عنهم, there is no doubt that it is an evidence, because among them are the righteous caliphs whom النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 2 ordered us to follow, and because they are the best of generations from this (المسلم) nation. You should know that the difference of opinion regarding the number of Rak‘ahs in Tarawih and the like is in a matter in which it is مباح to exercise الاجتهاد. It is undesirable that it should be a cause of differing and schism between the people, especially since the Salaf differed in the matter. There is nothing in the matter to prevent the exercise of الاجتهاد. How fine is the saying of one of the العلماء, when a person differed with him in his الاجتهاد in a matter in which it was مباح: By _ contradicting me, you have agreed with me, for both of us consider that it is واجب to follow what one sees to be the ''l Reported by Malik in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter on the Night الصلاة in Ramadan (280). الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 512 PRY OI I sobs truth, since it is مباح to exercise Jjtihad. We ask الله, the Most High that He grant us all success in attaining that which He loves and is pleasing to Him. Q. 280. What is the ruling on making the الدعاء for completing the recitation of the Qur’an during the night الصلاة in the month of Ramadan?
